seo.title: Building Research Agents with Mastra + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with Mastra + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Mastra provides a powerful TypeScript framework for building sophisticated AI agents with workflows, knowledge bases, and seamless third-party integrations, making it ideal for research agents that perform web search, analysis, and synthesis. However, multi-step processes introduce risks like unvalidated outputs, malformed tool responses, stale memory retrievals, and sync data confusion that can compromise accuracy and reliability. Integrating Vex as middleware ensures robust guardrails for source verification, claim accuracy, recency checks, and bias detection, safeguarding your research outputs.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with Mastra
architectureOverview.description:
Research agents in Mastra typically follow a multi-step workflow: starting with a query router that determines search strategy, followed by parallel web search tools that gather sources from multiple engines. Retrieved content flows through a validation layer checking recency and source credibility, then into an analysis agent that extracts claims and detects biases. Finally, a synthesis agent compiles findings into structured summaries with citations, storing results in vector memory for future retrieval.

The architecture leverages Mastra's workflow engine for orchestration, with tools handling search APIs (Serper, Tavily), knowledge retrieval from Pinecone/Weaviate, and LLM chains for analysis. Memory management ensures context persistence across sessions, while sync integrations pull real-time data from news APIs or databases. Vex middleware intercepts data at critical transitions for verification.

Key components include agent executors, tool registries, and workflow transitions, all extensible via Mastra's plugin system.

risks.name: Workflow step outputs not validated between transitions
risks.description: Search results pass to analysis without format/structure validation, causing LLM parsing failures
risks.mitigation: Vex middleware validates JSON schema compliance at each workflow transition

risks.name: Agent tool calls returning malformed data
risks.description: Search APIs return inconsistent HTML/JSON structures that break downstream claim extraction
risks.mitigation: Vex normalizes tool outputs to standardized formats before agent consumption

risks.name: Memory retrieval injecting stale context
risks.description: Old research results retrieved for current queries, mixing temporal contexts
risks.mitigation: Vex recency checks filter memory results by publication date and query timestamp

risks.name: Sync integration data causing agent confusion
risks.description: Real-time news feeds with breaking/changing stories confuse analysis agents
risks.mitigation: Vex flags volatile content and triggers re-verification workflows
